name: GradAlign: Gradient-Aligned Data Selection for LLM Reinforcement Learning

description: arXiv:2602.21492v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a central post-training paradigm for large language models (LLMs), but its performance is highly sensitive to the quality of training problems. This sensitivity stems from the non-stationarity of RL: rollouts are generated by an evolving policy, and learning is shaped by exploration and reward feedb...
author: Ningyuan Yang, Weihua Du, Weiwei Sun, Sean Welleck, Yiming Yang
